hero.title: 7 Days, Tamil Nadu.
hero.titleAccent: Temple Towns, French Coast and Hill Country.
hero.intro: The grand sweep of Tamil Nadu in a week. From Chennai's coast and the shore temples of Mahabalipuram to the great temple cities of Thanjavur and Madurai, with French Pondicherry and the cool Kodaikanal hills along the way.
hero.heroImageAlt: Tamil Nadu temple towns and Meenakshi temple

overview.heading: 7 Days Tamil Nadu Tour: The Full Picture
body: Tamil Nadu is the temple heartland of India, and this 7 day tour captures its full range: the Dravidian temple cities, the Coromandel coast, French colonial Pondicherry, and a touch of hill country. It is a journey through Tamil culture, devotion and history at a comfortable pace.

Arranged privately from Malaysia, the trip links Chennai, Mahabalipuram, Pondicherry, the great Chola city of Thanjavur and the temple metropolis of Madurai, with your own vehicle and driver guide. Towering gopurams, living rituals and a constantly changing landscape make Tamil Nadu one of the most rewarding states to explore.

highlights.heading: Why Travellers from Malaysia Love This Trip

items.title: Madurai's Meenakshi Temple
items.body: The vast Meenakshi Amman Temple, with its towering, sculpture-covered gopurams and thousand-pillared hall, is one of the most spectacular temple complexes in all of India.

items.title: Thanjavur's Chola Heritage
items.body: The magnificent Brihadeeswarar Temple, a UNESCO World Heritage Site and a masterpiece of Chola architecture, with its soaring vimana tower.

items.title: Mahabalipuram & Pondicherry
items.body: The seaside Pallava monuments of Mahabalipuram and the pastel French Quarter of Pondicherry add coastal heritage and colonial charm to the temple trail.

days.title: Arrive Chennai
days.description: Met at Chennai airport and transferred to your hotel. Evening at Marina Beach.

days.title: Chennai to Pondicherry via Mahabalipuram
days.description: Shore temples at Mahabalipuram, then on to French Pondicherry.

days.title: Pondicherry to Thanjavur
days.description: Pondicherry sights, then drive to the Chola city of Thanjavur.

days.title: Thanjavur to Madurai
days.description: Temple stops en route to Madurai.

days.title: Madurai
days.description: A full day at the great temple city.

days.title: Madurai to Kodaikanal
days.description: Drive up to the cool hill station of Kodaikanal.

days.title: Depart
days.description: Descend and transfer to Madurai airport for your onward flight.

hotelOptions.heading: Choose Your Comfort Level
hotelOptions.subheading: Centrally located hotels with easy access to the main sights. Pick the tier that suits your style.

whyBook.heading: Tamil Nadu, Threaded Together
whyBook.body: Tamil Nadu's temple cities are spread across the state, and the rituals reward understanding. We route the journey and pair you with knowledgeable guides. Dhesu has run South India tours since 1988.

points.title: MATTA & IATA Registered
points.body: Fully licensed Malaysian travel agency. We guide you through India e-Visa documentation with every booking.

points.title: Private Tours, Daily Departures
points.body: No minimum group to wait for. Your trip runs on your dates, your pace, your own vehicle and guide.

points.title: A Real Team to Call
points.body: Reachable throughout your trip. Flight delayed, your guide waits. Plans change, we adapt on the ground.

points.title: Group & Custom Pricing
points.body: Travelling as a family or group lowers the per-person cost. Ask us for a tailored quote.

sections.heading: Tamil Nadu Tour from Malaysia
sections.body:
This 7 day tour is the ideal introduction to Tamil Nadu, India's temple heartland. It links the coast, the great temple cities, French Pondicherry and a hill station, capturing the state's culture and devotion at a comfortable pace.

A private vehicle and driver guide handle the considerable distances and bring the temple rituals and history to life.

For a temple-focused pilgrimage, see our Arupadai Veedu and Navagraha packages; for a wider south loop, the 9 day Best of South India.

sections.heading: The Temple Cities of Tamil Nadu
sections.body:
Tamil Nadu's Dravidian temples are among the greatest in India. Madurai's Meenakshi Temple, with its towering painted gopurams, and Thanjavur's UNESCO-listed Brihadeeswarar Temple are highlights, alongside Srirangam, one of the largest functioning temple complexes in the world.

These are living temples, full of daily ritual, music and devotion, which makes exploring them with a guide so rewarding.

items.question: Do Malaysians need a visa for India?
items.answer: Yes. Malaysian passport holders apply for an India e-Visa online before departure. The tourist e-Visa covers tourism and short visits and is valid for 30 days from entry. The application is on the official India e-Visa portal, and Dhesu walks you through it when you book. Visa fees are not included in the package price.

items.question: Which airport does this trip use?
items.answer: This is a land-only package: hotels, transfers, tours and guide are included, but international flights are not. Most Malaysian travellers fly from Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) to Chennai or Madurai Airport (MAA / IXM). We can recommend suitable flights and timings on request.

items.question: What is the best time for a Tamil Nadu tour?
items.answer: November to February is the most comfortable, with cooler, drier weather. March to June is hot and humid. The northeast monsoon brings rain to Tamil Nadu around October to December. Winter is ideal for the temples and the coast.

items.question: Is there a dress code for the temples?
items.answer: Yes. Modest dress covering shoulders and knees is expected, and you remove footwear before entering. Some temples require men to remove shirts at the inner sanctum. Your guide will advise on each temple's specific customs.

items.question: How much driving does this tour involve?
items.answer: Tamil Nadu's sights are spread across the state, so there are several scenic drives. The seven-day pace keeps each day comfortable, and the private vehicle makes the longer legs relaxing with stops along the way.
